spec_match.rationale: The token inspector accurately breaks down base64url sections of the EVP token, illustrating the JWT structural expectations.

interactive.rationale: Fully interactive with buttons to clear, load dynamic server-signed samples, and inspect parsed claims dynamically.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the Relying Party explanation of reading the hidden input field containing the cryptographic token.

edge_cases.rationale: Ensures malformed input and syntax/JSON parsing errors are surfaced cleanly to the user.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com feature ID correctly along with the GitHub explainer.

design_system.rationale: Conforms properly to CSS variables, AA color contrast ratios, and utilizes native focusable layout buttons.

openQuestions.title: Deduplicate with Token Decoder
openQuestions.detail: The Token Inspector and Token Decoder concepts are extremely similar in purpose, both decoding base64url parts and presenting JWT claims. Consider merging them into a single, high-fidelity decoder/inspector tool, or differentiate them further (e.g., focus Token Inspector on real-time live debugging and Token Decoder on detailed spec description).

summary: The token-inspector is a highly compliant, interactive tool to verify and dissect JWT token payloads, using real server-signed backend samples. It also includes the required ChromeStatus links.
